data.body:
RESEARCH FINDINGS (2026-07-10 design session) — moving to `idea`: premise not yet supported by evidence.

## Empirical evidence (full REVIEW_RESULT sidecar population)
Mined ALL plan-review sidecars: 1,177 plan_review_result_v1 events (1,152 real LLM runs) across 371 tickets, 14,546 surfaced findings, 1,011 multi-finding reviews, 238 tickets with 2+ rounds.

- **G-1 (contradictory coaching): ZERO genuine instances.** No single review coached the same target in two opposite directions. The canonical tension pair G5 ("split/reduce") x G3 ("under-covers/add") NEVER co-surfaced on a shared location. All 138 heuristic candidate pairs were false positives — complementary refinements ("append to scope" + "add strip pass"; "relocate write" + "wrap in try/except"), not oppositions. The absence looks STRUCTURAL, not sampling: rebar's coach renders deterministic templates over independently-scoped criteria, so it cannot emit freely-contradictory prose the way DSO's free-form persona committee (its source of the G-1 pattern) could — which is precisely the DSO failure mode rebar's architecture was built to avoid. => The epic's own gating SC4 ("evidence that contradictory coaching actually occurs before building") returns a clean NO.

- **G-2 (oscillation): real but rare — 17 true flaps across 4 tickets (~1.7%).** A finding vanishes then reappears against a BYTE-IDENTICAL plan (review nondeterminism), e.g. 0f70 norm nc22480f8 (T5b) surfaced r2, dropped r3, resurfaced r4 on unchanged plan text; plus a severity flap minor->major under identical input (cce1, T3). Raw 74 flags decomposed to: 4 aborted-round artifacts, 53 plan-edit/revert-explained (correct fingerprint tracking — incl. the two highest-count tickets 5a8f/e1f4 which edited-then-reverted to byte-identical plans), 17 true same-plan flaps. The data to detect this is ALREADY in the sidecar (norm_ids, RETAIN_PER_TICKET=10, loadable at review time).

## Architecture findings (grounding the detection-level decision, if ever revived)
- The coach MOVE_REGISTRY moves are mostly NON-directional process nudges (spike, pre-mortem, research); only #10 (split/reduce) and #11 (add-to-children) encode a direction. So the gap report's cheap "detect opposing pairs over the coach (move,subject) tuples" is weak — a finding's direction lives in criterion semantics (G5=reduce, G3=expand, T4=add-compat, A1=remove) and suggested_fix prose, NOT the chosen move.
- rebar's `location` is FREE PROSE (not a structured target). The only reliable structured cross-finding join key is `criteria: list[str]` (rubric ids). So any target/direction join realistically comes from a curated known-tension criterion-pair table and/or an LLM adjudication.
- Best insertion point (if revived): the deterministic floor region of `_run_plan_review` (`__init__.py` ~455-469), alongside the rising/completion floors, after partition; prior-run data already loadable via `sidecar.latest_review_result`. Best precedent for an additive verdict block: the gated, advisory-only `overlap[]` post-step (`__init__.py` ~503-518). A new `conflicts` key needs a `plan_review_verdict.schema.json` update.

## DSO mechanism + prior art (for the record)
- DSO Stage 2 is an LLM SELF-SCAN emitting a `conflicts[]` array (not a separate deterministic pass); the resolution ladder is deterministic caller code. FP discipline = two hard gates: same `target` AND both findings >= major (minor-vs-anything is definitionally not a conflict).
- Prior-art SOTA is a HYBRID: cheap high-recall filter (deterministic direction-tags / NLI) to propose opposing pairs, LLM only to adjudicate survivors. Pure NLI over-flags (37% precision -> 92% with LLM on top; LegalWiz 2510.03418). Dominant FP trap = surface-opposed-but-different-target => group by CONCRETE target first (requirement-contradiction study s10515-024-00452-x).

## Recommendation (for when/if promoted idea->open)
1. G-1 detection: DO NOT build an LLM conflict pass now — speculative machinery for a defect class never observed in 1,011 reviews (the YAGNI/spent-optionality pattern rebar's own A1 flags). If ever desired, ship only a ZERO-COST DET tripwire: a known-tension criterion-pair table (G5xG3, T4xA1) emitting an advisory `conflicts` block IF two opposed-direction criteria ever co-surface on the same location — a live canary; its first firing becomes the evidence to justify a richer detector.
2. G-2 oscillation: this is where the (small) real value + a near-free implementation are — a read-side annotation (`oscillating: true` + coach note) over existing sidecar norm_ids, no new LLM call, no blocking change. Worth spinning into its own designed ticket independent of G-1.

Analysis artifacts (uncommitted, this session): /tmp/rr_all.json, /tmp/rr_analysis.pkl.
